title: Purpose of of highlights
html:
Why should I make highlights while reading? And what should be highlighted? How would they be used? Without an answer to these, highlighting quietly turns into a habit that feels productive while producing nothing.

The Types of Highlight

Resonate: the classic meaning of highlights. They are what connected me with the book, my owner personal feeling with them. A line lights up not because it’s objectively important, but because it touched something already in me. Idea for resonate highlight: telescoped / roll-able context.

Asterisks: to mark for ideas/concepts that need further delving for understanding. These aren’t keepsakes—they’re open loops, flags planted on the spots I owe more thought.

When to make Highlights

Highlights should serve a purpose for future action, not to collect from the fear of missing out. If a highlight has no future in it—nothing you’ll revisit, reuse, or act on—then you’re only hoarding sentences. (A distinction I borrowed from a friend’s thinking.)

  • A Reading System
  • The trap of information consumption without action
